metadata.file_name: 115. Rausch et al. (2024). Designing an International Large‑Scale Assessment of Professional Competencies and Employability Skills: Emerging Avenues and Challenges of OECD’s PISA‑VET.pdf
metadata.title: Designing an International Large-Scale Assessment of Professional Competencies and Employability Skills: Emerging Avenues and Challenges of OECD’s PISA-VET

survey_design.weight_fields_interpretation: This paper is a design and framework discussion of OECD’s PISA-VET assessment (development/pilot/implementation phases) and does not report an empirical student-level analysis. No dataset/cycle is analyzed and no analytic sample is constructed from PISA-VET microdata. Consequently, there is no description of complex survey weighting, replicate-weight methods, or any weight variable (e.g., W_FSTUWT, TOTWGT) being applied. The open-access article focuses on target populations, occupational areas, assessment approaches, and contextual/system-level questionnaires rather than estimation procedures.

data.handling_not_reported_explanation: PISA-VET is presented as an assessment framework under development, with proposed assessment approaches (e.g., scenario-based questions, digital simulations, live/recorded demonstrations) and contextual/system-level questionnaires. The paper does not conduct an empirical analysis of cognitive achievement outcomes that would require plausible values (PVs) or report any PV generation/combining strategy. Missing-data handling is also not applicable because no model estimation on an analytic dataset is performed; the article is methodological and agenda-setting rather than an evaluation study.

sample_details.sample_filtering_criteria: No student analytic sample is constructed. The paper outlines the target population (learners near completion of initial VET programmes) and describes planned assessment and contextual/system-level data collection for future PISA-VET implementation phases.

main_findings.standardized_conclusion: Using OECD the study leveraged the reported predictors to predict Literature synthesis outcome (not student-level prediction), finding that This is a non-empirical methodology paper describing the design of OECD’s PISA-VET assessment framework rather than reporting predictive modeling results. It outlines target learner groups (end of initial VET programmes), selected occupational areas (automotive technicians, electricians, business and administration, healthcare/nursing assistants, hotel receptionists), and the planned assessment approaches for professional skills and employability skills. The paper also proposes system-level and contextual questionnaires to support cross-country interpretation and equity/quality analyses. Because no student-level dataset is analyzed, no ML algorithms, performance metrics, or PV/missing-data handling strategies are evaluated in this article. This indicates that  support cross-country interpretation and equity/quality analyses. data.outcome_summary: This is a non-empirical methodology paper describing the design of OECD’s PISA-VET assessment framework rather than reporting predictive modeling results. It outlines target learner groups (end of initial VET programmes), selected occupational areas (automotive technicians, electricians, business and administration, healthcare/nursing assistants, hotel receptionists), and the planned assessment approaches for professional skills and employability skills. The paper also proposes system-level and contextual questionnaires to support cross-country interpretation and equity/quality analyses. Because no student-level dataset is analyzed, no ML algorithms, performance metrics, or PV/missing-data handling strategies are evaluated in this article.
data.null_fields_interpretation: The manuscript is a framework/design discussion of PISA-VET (development/pilot/implementation phases) and does not include an empirical large-scale assessment dataset, analytic sample, or any trained/evaluated ML models. Therefore, fields such as total_students, country-level sample sizes, PV handling, missing-data handling, and ML technique performance cannot be extracted from results tables because they are not present.
